prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging and improved diagnosis

vulnerablePopulations: patients without access to multidisciplinary ALS clinics
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical UMN/LMN signs with progression; EMG supportive; El Escorial/Awaji criteria; exclude mimics

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Not curable; survival and quality improved by multidisciplinary care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EMG and specialist neurology access concentrated in larger centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Disease-modifying therapy where available (riluzole), respiratory support (NIV), nutrition (PEG), physiotherapy, and palliative care planning
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited multidisciplinary ALS clinics and access to ventilatory support in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Tertiary centers can provide multidisciplinary care and palliative support
